title: rubus fruticosus var. rotunifolia
intro: Rubus fruticosus var. rotunifolia, also known as the blackberry or thimbleberry, is a type of deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help to protect against cell damage and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Rubus fruticosus var. rotunifolia include reducing the risk of heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer, as well as supporting digestive health and immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin irritations.
